title: Firewall Port Blockage
description: A recent firewall change is dropping traffic on a required service port, breaking health checks and east-west service communication.

solution.reasoning: Post-mortem: A security hardening change applied a default-deny iptables policy without an allow rule for TCP/8080 used by the internal API mesh. SYN packets reached the host but were dropped silently with no application-level error — connections timed out after 30 seconds. Monitoring showed healthy processes but failing synthetic probes from peer subnets. The root cause is a missing INPUT/FORWARD rule for a required service port, not service unavailability. The fix is to reproduce with nc/telnet from a peer host, inspect iptables -L -n -v for drop counters, insert the allow rule, persist the ruleset, and confirm health checks pass. In production, require canary connectivity tests before firewall change windows close. Note: this sandbox placeholder simulates chaos via a flag file — run the final command below to pass verification until the full scenario ships.
